content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 03:04 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct!

Please organize the technical debt discovered this week and create a technical debt tracking document, saved to the day07/ directory in Markdown format. Include entries with title, priority (high/medium/low), discovered_at, and estimated_effort fields.

arguments.content:
# Technical Debt Tracking (Week of 2026-03-16 → 2026-03-24)

This document captures technical debt identified during this week’s engineering discussions and the 2026-03-23 API review.

## Debt Entries

| Title | Priority | Discovered_at | Estimated_effort |
|---|---|---:|---:|
| v1 endpoint surface area is difficult to maintain (growing complexity, fragile changes) | high | 2026-03-23 | 2–4 weeks |
| Missing standardized API deprecation signaling (Sunset/Deprecation headers + docs links) | high | 2026-03-23 | 3–5 days |
| Inconsistent authentication model across versions; v2.3 requires OAuth2 + JWT but shared middleware is not yet in place | high | 2026-03-23 | 1–2 weeks |
| Lack of tier-aware rate limiting infrastructure at the edge (needs Redis-backed counters + headers + 429 model) | medium | 2026-03-23 | 4–7 days |
| No dedicated v2.3 staging environment / E2E test pipeline for auth + rate limit verification | medium | 2026-03-23 | 2–4 days |
| Frontend SDK needs updates to support v2.3 auth flow (client adoption blocker) | medium | 2026-03-23 | 3–5 days |
| Migration documentation/tooling gap for v1 → v2 (guide + FAQ + endpoint mapping) | high | 2026-03-23 | 5–8 days |

## Notes
- Estimated efforts are rough-order-of-magnitude and should be revisited after initial implementation spikes.
- Owners and target sprint placement can be added once the backlog is triaged with PM.

content.text:
Saved the technical debt tracking document to:

- `day07/20260324_technical_debt_tracking.md`
